questions.question: The file system structure typically includes the following components
questions.explanation: Inodes are used to store metadata about files and directories, such as ownership and permissions.

questions.question: A file system is typically divided into the following levels
questions.explanation: The file system is divided into logical levels, such as file level and directory level.

questions.question: The root directory is the top-most directory in the file system
questions.explanation: The root directory is the top-most directory in the file system hierarchy.

questions.question: In a file system, directories are used to organize files
questions.explanation: Directories are used to organize files and subdirectories in a file system.

questions.question: File systems can be classified into the following types
questions.explanation: File systems can be classified into hierarchical, network, and inverted types.
